books.title: US Wall Map (US students only)
books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: This map clearly shows landforms, and its projection is more accurate than some other maps.
books.noteText: Hang map at student eye level.
books.maySubText: any US wall map that shows both geographical features and place names. Avoid maps with roadways, as these obscure other features.
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grades 1-3
Resources: Grades 4-6
Resources: Grades 7-8

books.title: Canadian Wall Map (CA students only)
books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: This map clearly shows landforms, and its projection is more accurate than some other maps.
books.noteText: Hang map at student eye level.
books.maySubText: any Canadian wall map that shows both geographical features and place names. Avoid maps with roadways, as these obscure other features.
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grades 1-3
Resources: Grades 4-6
Resources: Grades 7-8

books.title: World Wall Map
books.author: National Geographic
books.rationale: The projection of this wall map distorts the image less than others. It also makes geographical features easy to find and place names easy to read.
books.noteText: Hang map at student eye level.
books.maySubText: any world wall map that shows both geographical features and place names

Resources: Grades 7-8

books.title: Handbook of Nature Study
books.author: Anna Comstock

books.maySubText: the full-color seven-volume set from Living Books Press
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grades 1-3
Resources: Grades 4-6
Resources: Grades 7-8

books.title: Nature Journaling for a Wild Life

books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: The Naturalist's Notebook
books.author: Nathaniel T. Wheelwright & Bernd Heinrich
books.rationale: Become a more attentive observer and deepen your appreciation for the natural world. The unique five-year calendar format of _The Naturalist’s Notebook_ helps you create a long-term record and point of comparison for memorable events.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Tasting History
books.author: Max Miller
books.rationale: Including the original recipe and Miller’s modern recreation, this cookbook is a must-have for any avid cook or history fan looking to experience delicious recipes from the past.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Baking Class
books.author: Deanna Cook
books.rationale: This book teaches the secrets of great baking, using kid-friendly directions and recipes.

books.title: Curwen Music
books.author: Mrs. Curwen & Kelli Rummel
books.rationale: These are free video piano lessons for beginners that use the Curwen method.
books.noteText: Preliminary and part of First Step lessons are available online; higher levels are incomplete.
books.maySubText:
local piano lessons. Curwen teacher preferred
➜ May also sub Hoffman Academy

books.title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
books.author: Samin Nosrat
books.rationale: Whether for afternoon occupations or High School Food Science, young cooks will be inspired by this well-written text with its engaging narrative and beautiful illustrations.  The author does a good job of introducing the basic ingredients of cooking without requiring a full chemistry course; students who have already taken chemistry will find additional connections to enhance their understanding even more.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)
Food Science Lessons

books.author: Tom Alphin
books.rationale: Travel through the history of architecture in _The LEGO Architect_. Learn about styles like Art Deco, Modernism, and High-Tech, and find inspiration in galleries of LEGO models. Then take a turn building 12 models in a variety of styles.
books.sharedText:

books.rationale: Whether for afternoon occupations or High School Food Science, young cooks will be inspired by this well-written text with its engaging narrative and beautiful illustrations.  The author does a good job of introducing the basic ingredients of cooking without requiring a full chemistry course; students who have already taken chemistry will find additional connections to enhance their understanding even more.
books.noteText: Available on Kindle with enhanced typesetting and Audible.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)
Food Science Lessons

books.title: The LEGO® Architect
books.author: Tom Alphin
books.rationale: Travel through the history of architecture in _The LEGO Architect_. Learn about styles like Art Deco, Modernism, and High-Tech, and find inspiration in galleries of LEGO models. Then take a turn building 12 models in a variety of styles.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: The LEGO® Engineer
books.author: Jeff Friesen
books.rationale: LEGO® bricks meet _The Way Things Work_ in this fun, informative tour of the world of engineering, from the creative mind of expert LEGO® builder Jeff Friesen.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Mastering Hand Building
books.author: Sunshine Cobb
books.rationale: From pinch pots to coiled boxes to soft slab tableware, mastering hand building is a lifelong pursuit. This book covers all the foundational skills, with lessons for constructing both simple and complex forms from clay.

books.title: GeoPuzzles
books.rationale: Kids will learn geography in a fun and engaging way, while discussing the different countries and how they are related.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Pottery for Beginners
books.author: Ruth Baldwin
books.rationale: Unleash your inner creativity and master the art of pottery with this complete craft guide to the wonders of ceramics.

books.title: The Master Theorem: A Book of Puzzles, Intrigue and Wit
books.author: M
books.rationale: M's Theorems are not your average Sudoku or crossword puzzles. They're each completely unique, beautifully illustrated, "escape room"-like puzzles that’ll have you decrypting messages and tackling creative brain-teasers.

books.title: Periodic Table of Elements 1000 Piece Puzzle
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Mini-Farming: Self-Sufficiency on 1/4 Acre
books.author: Brett Markham
books.rationale: This resource is for the more seasoned gardener who is interested in self-sufficiency and conservation.

books.title: The Virtual Instructor (Art)
books.rationale: This is a wonderful art teacher resource for students.

Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: High School Handbook
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: This manual covers everything from transcripts to testing and from college to trade careers (plus a whole lot more). Like an Alveary lesson plan, this book is simply a tool that is only effective when backed by Charlotte Mason’s philosophy of education.
books.sharedText:
Resources: Grade 9
Resources: Grade 10
Resources: Grade 11
Resources: Grade 12

books.title: Historical Atlas of the World 6th Ed.
books.author: Rand McNally
books.rationale: Presents important periods and turning points in 5,000 years of history. Thematic maps include literacy, languages, religions, and more. Used across curriculum.

books.title: United States Atlas 7th Ed.
books.author: National Geographic Kids
books.rationale: This atlas is used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.
books.noteText: Optional for Grade 1. For U.S. students.
books.maySubText: earlier edition. Other atlases may be used; however, map work and other lessons refer specifically to this one.
books.sharedText:

books.title: Nature Journaling for a Wild Life
books.author: Roseann Beggy Hanson
books.rationale: Explore the enriching world of nature journaling in an eight-week, self-guided “workshop in a book” from explorer, naturalist, and artist Roseann Hanson.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: The Naturalist's Notebook
books.author: Nathaniel T. Wheelwright & Bernd Heinrich
books.rationale: Become a more attentive observer and deepen your appreciation for the natural world. The unique five-year calendar format of _The Naturalist’s Notebook_ helps you create a long-term record and point of comparison for memorable events.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)

books.title: Tasting History
books.author: Max Miller
books.rationale: Including the original recipe and Miller’s modern recreation, this cookbook is a must-have for any avid cook or history fan looking to experience delicious recipes from the past.
books.sharedText:
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 1-8 (Opt.)
Afternoon Occupations: Grades 9-12 (Opt.)
